name: HoneyDrunk.Build
description: MSBuild props/targets, versioning, packaging, and deterministic output for all .NET Nodes. Stylistic rules now live in HoneyDrunk.Standards.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Build is the mechanical backbone of the Grid's .NET projects. It supplies shared MSBuild props/targets, semantic versioning, reproducible build settings, and packaging conventions so every artifact is traceable and identical regardless of machine or CI. Stylistic enforcement (formatting, naming, lint) is intentionally delegated to HoneyDrunk.Standards to keep Build lean and unambiguous.
long_description.why_it_exists: Eliminate build drift and ensure reproducible, auditable artifacts across all .NET Nodes.
long_description.primary_audience: Maintainers and service owners who compile, test, and publish .NET code.
value_props: One MSBuild grammar across repos
Reproducible builds & source link
Consistent versioning and packaging
Faster CI via centralized targets
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first; open-core patterns possible for public SDKs.

name: HoneyDrunk.Pipelines
description: Reusable CI/CD templates for Azure DevOps. Internal path with private feed auth, gated releases, package promotion.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Pipelines is the bloodstream of the Grid — every artifact, commit, and release flows through it. It codifies how things move from local intention to global availability, weaving together build, validation, promotion, and deployment under one unified grammar. Pipelines eliminate human drift in release discipline by encoding every pattern as YAML templates shared across the Hive. It carries identity, observability, and risk-awareness in every stage; no release leaves the lab without its own record of lineage and health. In the near term, Pipelines will unify Azure DevOps and GitHub Actions into one language, making hybrid orchestration seamless between private enterprise and public repositories. Later, it will tie into Pulse and Vault, becoming reflexive — capable of gating rollouts based on telemetry or automatically rolling back failed releases. The vision is an infrastructure that's not just continuous but conscious — aware of its own health, timing, and dependencies. Pipelines earns its keep by making velocity safe — transforming release anxiety into a repeatable dance.

name: HoneyDrunk.Kernel
description: Context propagation, lifecycle orchestration, and Grid primitives that power every Node across the Hive. The foundational runtime layer.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Kernel is the semantic OS layer of HoneyDrunk.OS, the Hive. It is the foundational runtime that nodes, agents and services use to communicate, coordinate and observe themselves across the Grid. Core responsibilities: Context propagation—a three tier model (GridContext, NodeContext, OperationContext) that flows across async boundaries and transports. Lifecycle orchestration—startup hooks, lifecycle stages, health and readiness aggregation, graceful shutdown. Configuration and identity primitives—strongly typed IDs at configuration time, string based identities at runtime, options for Grid and Node bootstrap. Telemetry integration—OpenTelemetry ready activity factory, trace enrichment and log correlation wired to Grid context. Health and readiness—health checks and lifecycle contributors for Kubernetes style /health and /ready endpoints. Agent interop—agent execution contexts, scoped context visibility and serializers that let higher level agent runtimes safely embed LLMs into the Grid. Kernel is the part of the Grid that defines what it means to be a node.
long_description.why_it_exists: To give every node the same runtime skeleton for identity, context, lifecycle and observability so those concerns are not reinvented in each service.
long_description.primary_audience: Node developers and service owners building Grid aware services. Agent runtimes and automation that need to execute with a proper Grid context.
value_props: Three tier context model—Grid to Node to Operation—with correlation and causation tracking
Lifecycle orchestration with startup hooks, lifecycle stages and contributor based health and readiness
OpenTelemetry ready tracing, enrichment and log scopes
Strongly typed identity at configuration time, string identity at runtime for performance
Kubernetes friendly health and readiness endpoints
Agent aware execution contexts and context serializers for LLM centric components
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal first. Public SDK patterns may be open core, but Kernel itself is the base runtime that all higher layers depend on.
long_description.roadmap_focus: v0.4 is a stabilization release. Enforce the three-ID context model across all creation paths. Consolidate hosting around AddHoneyDrunkNode as the canonical API. Align lifecycle, health and readiness behavior with docs. Lock identity registries as the DNS of the Grid. Tighten error classification and telemetry tag consistency. Finalize agent execution context contracts so AgentKit can build on stable primitives. Add local node self-description via INodeDescriptor and INodeManifest—no global registry yet.
long_description.demo_path: Minimal Node setup with AddHoneyDrunkGrid and AddHoneyDrunkNode → context flow across async calls → health probe validation → graceful shutdown with lifecycle hooks.

name: HoneyDrunk.Data
description: Persistence contracts, EF Core and SQL Server provider implementations, migrations discipline, tenant isolation, and diagnostics.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Data gives the Grid memory and continuity. It defines persistence contracts and provider conventions across every tenant, every node, and every environment, so storage stays predictable and auditable. This Node standardizes migrations, schema versioning, tenant isolation, and persistence telemetry. It is a guardrail against drift: the same operation should produce the same durable result, regardless of where it runs. Scope boundaries: Data persists facts. It does not contain business logic, and it does not publish messages. If an outbox is needed in the future, Data would own the storage schema and access patterns, while Transport owns dispatch and delivery behavior. As it matures, Data can expose optional hooks for Pulse to report schema health, migration status, and drift signals. Secrets and credentials are provided through the application's configuration pipeline, typically backed by Vault, without Data taking a direct dependency on Vault.
long_description.why_it_exists: HoneyDrunk.Data exists to prevent inconsistency and reinvention across the Grid.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk maintainers, contributors, and service owners.

Reduces operational risk with opinionated defaults
Improves developer velocity and interoperability
long_description.roadmap_focus: Advance HoneyDrunk.Data capabilities and harden contracts.

Pulse integration is optional for schema health and migration telemetry. Does not consume Vault directly (configuration provided at app layer). Does not consume Transport or Auth.

name: HoneyDrunk.Transport
description: Unified messaging abstraction over Azure Service Bus, Storage Queue, and in-memory brokers. Features middleware pipeline pattern, retry strategies, transactional outbox, and Grid context propagation.

long_description.overview: Transport is the messaging backbone for HoneyDrunk.OS applications. It provides transport-agnostic abstractions (ITransportPublisher, ITransportConsumer, IMessageHandler<T>) that enable broker-independent messaging. Built on an onion-style middleware pipeline similar to ASP.NET Core, it supports GridContext propagation, OpenTelemetry tracing, structured logging, and configurable retry strategies (Fixed, Linear, Exponential). The transactional outbox pattern ensures exactly-once delivery by storing messages within database transactions. Transport adapters for Azure Service Bus, Azure Storage Queue, and InMemory are available as separate packages.
long_description.why_it_exists: HoneyDrunk.Transport exists to decouple application logic from specific message broker implementations while providing enterprise-grade reliability patterns.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk service developers building distributed, event-driven applications.
value_props: Transport-agnostic messaging via ITransportPublisher/ITransportConsumer
Middleware pipeline for cross-cutting concerns (telemetry, logging, correlation)
Configurable retry strategies with backoff (Fixed, Linear, Exponential)
Transactional outbox pattern for exactly-once delivery
Grid context propagation with correlation and causation tracking
Health contributors for Kubernetes readiness/liveness probes
long_description.monetization_signal: Open source foundation; enterprise patterns emerge in implementations.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Advanced dead-letter strategies, per-subscription filters, and fault injection for testing.
long_description.grid_relationship: Uses Kernel's IGridContext, IOperationContext, and ITelemetryActivityFactory for correlation and telemetry. Provides Outbox contracts that Data bridges can implement.
long_description.demo_path: Publish a message via IMessagePublisher, observe middleware pipeline execution, verify handler invocation with Grid context.

long_description.overview: Web.Rest provides two packages: Abstractions (zero-dependency contracts for ApiResult, ApiErrorResponse, pagination, error codes) and AspNetCore (middleware for correlation ID propagation, exception-to-error mapping, auth failure shaping, and MVC/minimal API conventions). Together they ensure every REST API in the Grid returns consistent response envelopes with proper correlation tracking.
long_description.why_it_exists: Prevents inconsistent error shapes, missing correlation IDs, and divergent response formats across services. One AddRest()/UseRest() call configures everything.
long_description.primary_audience: ASP.NET Core service developers building REST APIs in the HoneyDrunk ecosystem.

name: HoneyDrunk.Vault
description: Secure secrets and configuration management. Zero-trust vault for sensitive data across environments.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Vault is the core secrets and configuration management library for HoneyDrunk.OS. This package provides the abstractions (ISecretStore, IConfigProvider, IVaultClient, ISecretProvider, IConfigSource), caching with TTL and sliding expiration, resilience policies (retry, circuit breaker), Kernel lifecycle integration, and distributed telemetry that all Vault providers plug into. Vault itself contains no provider implementations—it defines the contract, runtime behavior, and orchestration. Providers (File, Azure Key Vault, AWS, InMemory, Configuration) supply the actual secrets while Vault handles resilience, caching, and tracing.
long_description.why_it_exists: A unified, Kernel-aware interface for secrets and configuration—no matter where those values live. Prevents inconsistent secret handling and reinvention across the Grid.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk service developers and node owners who need secure, traceable access to secrets and configuration.

Secure telemetry (never logs secret values)

name: HoneyDrunk.Auth
description: Grid node component for authentication and authorization. Provides JWT bearer token validation with Vault-backed signing keys, deterministic policy evaluation, and deep Kernel integration for lifecycle and telemetry. Requires Kernel as host runtime.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Auth is a Grid node component—not a standalone library—that standardizes authentication and authorization decisions across nodes and entrypoints. It requires Kernel as its host runtime and provides JWT bearer token validation with Vault-backed signing keys, deterministic policy evaluation, and fail-fast startup validation. Auth ensures consistent identity checks and authorization patterns so every request entering the Grid follows the same security discipline. Key design constraints: authorization evaluation is local, deterministic, and side-effect free. Scope boundaries: Auth validates tokens and evaluates policies. It does not issue tokens, own user identity storage (that's app-specific), or perform OAuth/OIDC flows. Signing keys and auth configuration are provided through Vault. Over time, Auth can evolve to support passkeys, MFA, federated sign-ins, device trust, and context-aware authorization meshes. Future integration with Pulse for session observability and Sentinel for anomaly detection will enable dynamic policy adjustments based on behavior patterns and risk posture.
long_description.why_it_exists: Prevents inconsistent authentication and authorization patterns across nodes, ensuring security discipline is uniform regardless of entrypoint.
long_description.primary_audience: Service owners building HTTP APIs, gateways, and any Grid node requiring request authentication.
value_props: JWT bearer token validation with Vault-backed signing keys
Deterministic policy evaluation (local, side-effect free, auditable)
Fail-fast startup validation of required secrets
Deep Kernel integration for lifecycle hooks, telemetry, and context propagation
Clean separation between auth contracts (Abstractions) and implementations
long_description.monetization_signal: Open source foundation; enterprise patterns in implementations.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Passkeys and MFA support. Federated sign-ins and device trust. Pulse integration for session observability. Sentinel integration for anomaly detection. Context-aware authorization mesh.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (required) for GridContext, OperationContext, lifecycle hooks, and telemetry. Consumes Vault (required) for signing keys and auth configuration. Exposes auth contracts consumed by Web.Rest and service nodes via three packages: HoneyDrunk.Auth.Abstractions (pure contracts), HoneyDrunk.Auth (runtime), and HoneyDrunk.Auth.AspNetCore (ASP.NET Core integration).

The system defines category curves, prestige logic, and level schemas, ensuring that progress feels consistent across worlds. As a Core Node, HiveXP sets the laws of advancement, letting Market apps, Play titles, and Creator tools all speak the same language of effort and reward.
long_description.why_it_exists: To unify XP logic across the Hive, avoiding redundant or conflicting progression systems.
long_description.primary_audience: Developers, integrators, and any Node that needs measurable growth mechanics.
value_props: Centralized XP and level tracking.
Customizable category weighting per sector.
Seamless integration across Market and Creator products.
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal core Node; indirectly monetized through Market nodes using XP loops.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Expand XP events to support multi-category weighting and decay models.
long_description.grid_relationship: Connects with: HiveGigs, HoneyHub, HoneyPlay, MarketCore.
long_description.demo_path: Show XP transaction lifecycle: event → ledger → visible level change.
long_description.signal_quote: Growth is a pattern, not a prize.

name: HiveGigs
description: Micro-mission marketplace for real and digital worlds — AI-generated and user-authored Gigs that reward exploration, creativity, and community participation.

long_description.overview: HiveGigs turns the world into a living quest board. From local parks to online creative challenges, players and creators engage through short, rewarding missions called Gigs. AI agents seed the grid with procedurally written Gigs, while community members design and publish their own, earning payouts as others complete them. XP flows through HiveXP, rewards through MarketCore, and telemetry through Meta, forming a complete feedback loop of discovery and reward. HiveGigs is how the Hive leaves the screen and touches the world — one mission at a time.
long_description.why_it_exists: To create a self-sustaining ecosystem of micro-missions that reward exploration and creativity without needing constant manual curation.
long_description.primary_audience: Players, creators, and AI agents that populate the Hive with real and virtual missions.
value_props: Procedurally generated and community-authored missions.
XP and payout integration through HiveXP and MarketCore.
Expands Hive activity into real-world and hybrid contexts.
long_description.monetization_signal: Revenue share from sponsored or premium Gigs; creator commissions; possible event partnerships.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Integrate multi-agent authorship and dynamic quest templates per location.
long_description.grid_relationship: Connects with: HiveXP, Vault, MarketCore, Transport.
long_description.demo_path: Show HiveGigs flow: AI → published Gig → player completion → XP reward.

name: Meta.DevPortal
description: Public developer portal for HoneyDrunk.OS. Documentation, SDK guides, changelogs, and onboarding for developers building on the Grid.

long_description.overview: The public developer portal for HoneyDrunk.OS — documentation, SDK guides, changelogs, and onboarding surfaces for anyone building on the Grid.
long_description.why_it_exists: Developers and partners need a canonical, searchable source of truth for SDKs, APIs, and integration patterns. Meta.DevPortal unifies fragmented knowledge into a single entry point, ensuring consistency and discoverability.
long_description.primary_audience: External developers, contributors, and partner studios integrating with HoneyDrunk SDKs or APIs.
value_props: Unified documentation and changelogs across all SDKs.
Live examples linked to Pulse telemetry and Registry schema.
Open RFC index and version diff viewer for transparent evolution.
long_description.monetization_signal: Drives adoption of open-core SDKs; enables future pro-tier integration and partner licensing opportunities.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Automated doc generation via Registry and AtlasSync; introduce AI-assisted doc authoring and contextual search.
long_description.grid_relationship: Serves all public Nodes as the primary documentation and onboarding interface; sources metadata from Registry and Pulse.
long_description.demo_path: Navigate to developers.honeydrunkstudios.com → browse Node documentation → follow Quickstart flow for SDK setup.
long_description.signal_quote: Knowledge should travel as fast as code.

name: Meta.PackagePublisher
description: Automated release orchestrator for SDKs and shared libraries. Handles versioning, changelogs, signing, and multi-feed publication.

long_description.overview: Automated release orchestrator for SDKs and shared libraries — handles versioning, changelogs, signing, and multi-feed publication (NuGet, NPM, internal).
long_description.why_it_exists: Every Node that ships code requires a reliable, auditable, and standardized release pipeline. Manual publishing introduces drift and inconsistency across SDK ecosystems.
long_description.primary_audience: Core engineers, maintainers, and CI/CD pipeline owners responsible for versioned artifacts.
value_props: Semantic-version enforcement and automatic incrementing.
Auto-generated changelogs based on structured commit history.
Multi-feed distribution with rollback and audit support.
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal infrastructure; improves operational throughput and reduces release friction.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Integrate with Pulse metrics for post-release performance and adoption tracking; automate dependency graph updates across Nodes.
long_description.grid_relationship: Extends HoneyDrunk.Pipelines; reports release and artifact metadata to Registry and Pulse.
long_description.demo_path: Developer tags repo → Pipeline triggers release → packages published to feeds → Registry and Pulse updated.

name: Meta.AtlasSync
description: Neural synchronization layer connecting manifests, documentation, and agent memory. Keeps the Hive's collective knowledge coherent across mediums.

long_description.overview: Neural synchronization layer connecting manifests, documentation, and agent memory — keeps the Hive’s collective knowledge coherent across mediums.
long_description.why_it_exists: Context drifts rapidly between code, docs, and agent layers. AtlasSync detects divergence and restores alignment, ensuring the Hive’s data and understanding remain synchronized.
long_description.primary_audience: AI orchestration systems, documentation subsystems, and context-driven developer tools.
value_props: Federated context management across agents and systems.
Automated drift detection and regeneration of affected documents.
Feeds Meta.DevPortal and Console with real-time summaries.
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal alignment infrastructure; enables reliable agentic workflows and contextual accuracy.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Bidirectional sync between Notion, Registry, and Pulse metadata; add versioned memory maps for each sector.
long_description.grid_relationship: Bridges Meta.DevPortal, Audit.Agent, and Registry; acts as the knowledge circulatory system for Meta.
long_description.demo_path: Update manifest → AtlasSync detects drift → regenerates docs → Audit.Agent logs context replay.
long_description.signal_quote: Memory is the backbone of coherence.

name: MarketCore
description: Shared economic and payout logic for all Market-sector nodes. Pricing, royalties, XP monetization, and marketplace rules.

long_description.overview: Shared economic and payout logic for all Market-sector Nodes — pricing, royalties, XP monetization, and marketplace rules live here as unified services.
long_description.why_it_exists: As the Hive grows, multiple Market Nodes require consistent financial logic to ensure transparency, compliance, and predictable revenue flows.
long_description.primary_audience: Product teams using HiveXP, Payouts, and related financial Nodes.
value_props: Centralized pricing and fee governance across the ecosystem.
Royalty splits and payout orchestration with verifiable ledgers.
Transaction auditing and reconciliation through Ledger integration.
long_description.monetization_signal: Powers monetization for all Market-sector Nodes; ensures financial integrity and partner trust.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Add dynamic pricing models, revenue sharing APIs, and advanced audit reporting.
long_description.grid_relationship: Interfaces with HiveGigs, HiveXP, Payouts, and Ledger; acts as the financial authority for Market operations.
long_description.demo_path: Creator completes Gig → MarketCore computes split → Payout emitted via Stripe integration.
long_description.signal_quote: Every credit must trace its origin.

name: HoneyDrunk.Console
description: Studio control plane for the Hive. Operational cockpit where infrastructure nodes, deployments, and audit trails converge.

long_description.overview: Studio control plane for the Hive — the operational cockpit where infrastructure Nodes, deployments, and audit trails converge into one unified surface.
long_description.why_it_exists: Operators need real-time observability and direct control over live systems. HoneyDrunk.Console provides a cohesive interface to monitor, manage, and align all operational components of the Hive.
long_description.primary_audience: Studio operators, infrastructure maintainers, and on-call engineers.
value_props: Aggregated view of Vault, Pulse, Registry, and Transport health.
Feature-flag, configuration, and metadata management tools.
Integrated auditing for all privileged or automated actions.
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal reliability tool; improves uptime, traceability, and incident response velocity.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Introduce real-time Registry diff visualizations and Atlas job viewer for live debugging.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes internal APIs from Ops-sector Nodes; emits logs and audits to Pulse and Audit.Agent.
long_description.demo_path: Open Console → view sector health metrics → trigger deploy → Audit.Agent logs event context.
long_description.signal_quote: Control is not authority; it’s awareness in motion.

name: Audit.Agent
description: Agentic governance node that logs, reviews, and enforces transparency across all automated and human actions within the Hive.

long_description.overview: Agentic governance node that logs, reviews, and enforces transparency across all automated and human actions within the Hive.
long_description.why_it_exists: As agent autonomy expands, oversight becomes essential. Audit.Agent provides accountability, ensuring every action has a traceable origin and ethical record.
long_description.primary_audience: Operators, ethics reviewers, and AI orchestration layers maintaining compliance and transparency.
value_props: Immutable audit logs for both AI and human-initiated actions.
Prompt and response context capture for explainability.
Replay engine for reconstructing decisions and enforcing policy.
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal compliance infrastructure; ensures operational trust and auditability.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Integrate with AtlasSync for contextual event replay and automated ethics checks.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes activity logs from Console, Pulse, and AgentKit; exposes read APIs to Ops and Meta layers.
long_description.demo_path: Agent executes task → Audit.Agent captures prompt and output → reviewer replays context → compliance verified.
long_description.signal_quote: Transparency is the truest form of control.

name: HoneyDrunk.Operator
short: Internal control plane for operating the Hive.
description: Human-operated control plane for observing system health, managing operational intent, and applying controlled interventions across all HoneyDrunk nodes and environments. Operator does not participate in governance, deliberation, or simulation-it defines and enforces operational policy, not decisions.

long_description.overview: Operator is the internal control plane for HoneyDrunk Studios. It provides a single, secure interface for observing system health, managing operational intent, and applying controlled interventions across all HoneyDrunk nodes and environments. Operator does not participate in governance, deliberation, or simulation. It defines and enforces operational policy, not decisions. Its purpose is to give humans a safe, auditable way to operate a growing multi-node system without embedding privileged controls into product nodes.
long_description.why_it_exists: To separate system operation from system behavior. As HoneyDrunk grows beyond a single node, operational concerns like safety controls, rollouts, cost limits, and incident response must be centralized. Operator exists so these concerns do not leak into Governor, Draft, or other market nodes-preserving clean boundaries, tenant isolation, and trust.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk founders and operators, internal platform maintainers, and on-call engineers and incident responders. Operator is not customer-facing.
value_props: Provides unified visibility across nodes, environments, and deployments.

Owns authoritative operational intent: agent role enablement, model and provider allowlists, cost ceilings and rate limits, safety modes (normal, degraded, read-only), per-environment and per-tenant circuit breakers.
Enables safe, audited human intervention during incidents.
Coordinates operational changes without redeploys or ad-hoc scripts.
Records who changed what, when, why, and with what scope.
long_description.monetization_signal: None. Operator is internal platform infrastructure. Its value is measured in reduced incidents, faster recovery, lower cognitive load, and safer scaling-not revenue.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Minimal, hardened control state schemas. Strong RBAC and audit trails. Safe change workflows with explicit scope and blast radius. Tight Pulse integration for cause-effect visibility. Deliberate resistance to feature creep or advisor behavior. Principle: boring, predictable, and trustworthy.
long_description.grid_relationship: Governor consumes operational intent defined by Operator-Operator never influences Governor's reasoning or outcomes. AgentKit enforces restrictions and permissions derived from Operator-defined control state. Operator consumes Pulse telemetry to inform human decision-making and validate impact of operational changes. Operator relies on Kernel for lifecycle, context propagation, and health aggregation. Operator observes and constrains the system-it does not steer content or strategy.
long_description.demo_path: Incident detected via Pulse -> On-call engineer opens Operator console -> Reviews system state and blast radius -> Applies circuit breaker to affected tenant via audited control change -> Monitors recovery metrics -> Documents resolution with full audit trail.
long_description.signal_quote: Control without opinion. Authority without authorship.
does_not_do: Does not generate plans or proposals.
Does not act as a persona or advisor.
Does not access customer governance content by default.

name: HoneyDrunk.Standards
description: Language-agnostic standards: .editorconfig/.globalconfig, lint configs, naming rules, and contribution etiquette applied across the Hive.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Standards is the Hive's shared discipline layer — a lightweight repository of style guides and static configurations that keep every codebase readable and consistent. It ships .editorconfig/.globalconfig, analyzer rule selections, ESLint/Prettier presets, commit etiquette, and PR guidance so teams write with one voice even across languages and engines.
long_description.why_it_exists: Prevent stylistic drift, reduce review noise, and make repos feel first-party the moment they're cloned.
long_description.primary_audience: All contributors across C#, TS/JS, and docs.
value_props: One set of rules across all repos
No secrets or feeds; safe as public submodule
Cuts PR noise by standardizing nits up front
long_description.monetization_signal: N/A — cultural backbone that improves velocity and perceived quality.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Expand beyond C# and TS to shaders/Unreal configs; add pre-commit hooks; publish a small handbook.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumed by every Node as a submodule or read-only folder; Build applies mechanical enforcement; Pipelines verify conformance.
long_description.demo_path: Add submodule at /.hd/standards → open solution → see consistent analyzer and formatting behavior immediately.

name: HoneyDrunk.Collector
description: Telemetry collector for distributed ingestion of logs, traces, and metrics from all services into Pulse.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Collector is the inbound nerve ending of the Hive — a telemetry sidecar that ingests and normalizes data from every service before relaying it to Pulse. It receives traces, metrics, and logs through OTLP and custom channels, enforcing schema, correlation, and deduplication at the edge. Collector ensures the Grid's telemetry remains consistent and cost-efficient, buffering intelligently and self-healing under load.
long_description.why_it_exists: Distributed systems need a standardized ingestion layer to prevent telemetry drift and ensure Pulse remains a single source of operational truth.
long_description.primary_audience: Service owners and ops engineers integrating observability into HoneyDrunk Nodes.
value_props: Unified OTLP and internal telemetry ingestion
Schema enforcement and tag normalization
Self-healing buffer and queue management
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal backbone; forms foundation for future observability SaaS offerings.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Add eBPF-based trace capture and auto-routing to regional Pulse instances.
long_description.grid_relationship: Feeds Pulse; integrates with Vault for auth and Transport for message relay.
long_description.demo_path: Run Collector sidecar → send traces → verify in Pulse dashboard.

name: HoneyDrunk.Comms
description: Unified outbound communication system for email, push, SMS, and webhooks across the Hive.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Comms (Notify) is the Grid's voice to the outside world — the outbound layer that handles all communication across channels: email, push, SMS, and webhook events. It provides standardized templates, rate-limiting, and delivery tracking for both internal and user-facing notifications. Comms guarantees that every signal sent by the Hive — from release alerts to agent updates — follows the same secure and aesthetic conventions.
long_description.why_it_exists: Without unified comms infrastructure, user notifications and internal alerts drift in tone, reliability, and security.
long_description.primary_audience: Developers and maintainers needing safe, branded, auditable outbound communication.
value_props: Unified templating for all outbound channels
Secure delivery with Vault-managed credentials
Pulse-linked tracking and retries for reliability
long_description.monetization_signal: Future SaaS or partner notification integrations (SendGrid, Twilio, FCM).
long_description.roadmap_focus: Introduce webhook orchestration and adaptive send policies based on Pulse metrics.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes events from Transport; integrates with Vault and Pulse for delivery audit.
long_description.demo_path: Trigger workflow → Comms sends templated message → view telemetry in Pulse.
long_description.signal_quote: When the Hive speaks, it's heard everywhere.

name: HoneyDrunk.Assets
description: Cross-Node storage and access control for digital assets, media, and documents across the Grid.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Assets is the Hive's memory vault — a centralized storage and distribution layer for all digital artifacts: images, videos, binaries, and documents. It enforces access control, lifecycle policies, and content-addressed versioning, ensuring every asset in the Grid is traceable and immutable. Assets integrates with Vault for encryption, Pulse for usage metrics, and Transport for replication events, forming the foundation for secure and auditable media delivery.
long_description.why_it_exists: Every system eventually needs to store and serve media securely. Assets unifies these patterns across Nodes instead of reinventing S3 wrappers and CDN hacks.
long_description.primary_audience: Developers and services requiring consistent, compliant asset handling.
value_props: Secure storage with Vault encryption
Signed URL delivery and CDN compatibility
Event-driven replication via Transport
long_description.monetization_signal: Potential open-core file service or premium storage tier.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Add content deduplication, preview generation, and CDN integrations.
long_description.grid_relationship: Connects with: Vault, Transport, Pulse.
long_description.demo_path: Upload asset → retrieve signed URL → view metrics in Pulse.

name: HoneyDrunk.Clarity
short: Context-aware intelligence assistant for creators and studios.
description: Advisory intelligence assistant for developers, creators, and small studios. Clarity helps humans understand systems, architecture, tools, and next steps by translating HoneyDrunk-native knowledge into clear, actionable guidance. Clarity explains, teaches, and proposes-it does not execute, govern, simulate, or operate systems.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Clarity is an advisory intelligence assistant for developers, creators, and small studios. It helps humans understand systems, architecture, tools, and next steps by translating HoneyDrunk-native knowledge, manifests, and best practices into clear, actionable guidance. Clarity explains, teaches, and proposes. It does not execute, govern, simulate, or operate systems.
long_description.why_it_exists: Modern creators are overwhelmed by fragmented documentation, generic copilots, and constant context switching. Clarity exists to provide situated understanding: answers that are grounded in the actual stack, patterns, and constraints the user is working within. Unlike general-purpose copilots, Clarity is opinionated toward HoneyDrunk's architecture and workflows while remaining advisory-only.
long_description.primary_audience: Indie developers, technical artists, designers, small studios, and product-focused creators using HoneyDrunk or compatible stacks.
value_props: Explains HoneyDrunk concepts such as Nodes, Flows, Vault, Transport, and Pipelines.
Answers 'why' and 'how' questions about architecture and tradeoffs.
Drafts artifacts such as ADRs, RFCs, proposals, migration plans, and implementation outlines.
Provides contextual guidance based on explicitly granted user context (repo, file, task, doc).
Summarizes telemetry and system behavior in human-readable terms.
Recommends when governance (Governor) or simulation (Sim) should be involved.
Always operates as a thinking partner, not a decision-maker.
long_description.monetization_signal: High. Free or bundled tier for individual creators. Pro tiers for studios: private knowledge ingestion, team-level memory, organization-wide patterns, multi-seat management, and richer context tooling. Clarity is a product. It earns trust by never overstepping.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Phase 1: Editor and browser surfaces (VS Code, JetBrains, web) with HoneyDrunk-native language and concepts. Phase 2: Cross-tool context (engines, DCC tools), team memory, and doc-grounded reasoning. Phase 3: Assisted workflows-draft Operator requests, prepare Governor submissions, explain Sim simulation results. All actions remain human-approved and auditable.
long_description.grid_relationship: AgentKit: Clarity uses AgentKit to route advisory tasks to specialized agents (language, domain expertise, drafting), without granting execution authority. Governor: Clarity may suggest that a question or proposal be submitted to Governor for multi-agent deliberation-Governor owns decisions. Sim: Clarity may request simulations and explain their outcomes once Sim exists-Sim produces evidence, Clarity narrates it. Operator: Clarity may draft or propose operational changes, but Operator is the only surface through which real operational intent is applied. Pulse: Clarity may summarize Pulse telemetry for humans but does not interpret telemetry as authority.
long_description.demo_path: Open project in IDE -> Clarity detects stack and context -> Ask how to structure a feature or understand a tradeoff -> Clarity explains using HoneyDrunk patterns and actual repo context -> Optionally draft an RFC or ADR for human review -> Never applies changes directly.
does_not_do: Does not disable agents or nodes.
Does not override governance or policy.
Clarity may propose. Clarity may draft. Clarity may explain. Clarity may request action. Clarity never acts.
context_safety: All context access is explicit and user-scoped.
No implicit access to secrets, production state, or privileged controls.
Vault is used only for securing API keys and encrypted user context.
Tenant and environment boundaries are strictly enforced.

name: HoneyDrunk.HomeLab
description: HoneyDrunk.HomeLab is the physical backbone of the Grid inside tatteddev’s home. It operates as a self-hosted storage, networking, and virtualization environment for experimentation, backups, and internal services. This node provides the local mirror of the Grid where Vault, Pulse, Collector, Transport, and Auth patterns can be validated safely at zero cloud cost.
purpose: Provide a physical Grid shard for storage, networking, and virtualization.
Enable expandable local storage and automated backup strategies.
Serve as the canonical sync hub across personal and studio devices.
Host media services, VMs, containers, and lab clusters.
Offer a safe environment for testing Vault, Pulse, Collector, and Transport deployments before cloud rollout.
responsibilities: Maintain NAS storage with snapshots and off-site replication.
Run device syncing layers and personal cloud services.
Manage home-scale VLANs, firewall rules, and local DNS.
Host Pulse agents, Collector sidecars, and Vault prototypes.
Run containers/VMs for experimental services and media servers.
Document network topology and operational runbooks.
vision: A fully mapped miniature Grid running on-prem: scalable NAS, local cluster, agent network, telemetry collectors, and secure remote access. HomeLab becomes the blueprint for future HoneyDrunk infrastructure patterns.

name: HoneyDrunk.Governor
description: The Neural Senate of the Grid. Hosts the multi-agent Board for strategy and orchestrates Node Agents for execution so that Intent can survive Reality (Sim).

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Governor is the deliberation layer of the Hive. It is the dedicated space where agents debate, govern, and align. It instantiates the Board (roles derived from Sector philosophies) to weigh strategic RFCs and summons Node Agents (derived from nodes.json) to handle tactical execution.
long_description.why_it_exists: To prevent architectural drift and generic hallucinated decisions by subjecting major changes to structured, multi-perspective review and deliberation.
long_description.primary_audience: The Founder, internal systems, and automated orchestration layers that need a source of strategic consensus.
value_props: Hosts a role based Board (CTO, COO, CISO, CPO) to debate strategy using Sector doctrine as case law
Dynamically spins up Node Agents based on nodes.json context and dependencies
Routes decisions into Sim for simulation before they are treated as real commitments
Logs every consensus event into Audit.Agent for immutable history and replay
long_description.monetization_signal: Brain feature for higher HoneyDrunk tiers. Studios and serious teams unlock Governor for structured deliberation and strategic consensus.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Define Board roles and their ties to sectors, integrate Sim as the simulation backend, and implement a Debate Protocol with voting, veto, and revision states.
long_description.grid_relationship: Sits above AgentKit and the Node layer, validates strategic intent, calls Sim to test outcomes, and reports strict logs to Audit.Agent. Consumes operational constraints defined by Operator.
long_description.demo_path: User submits an RFC. Governor convenes the Board. CTO and CISO debate risk versus structure, CPO argues market impact, Sim simulates branches, Governor records consensus and returns an approved plan for execution.

name: HoneyDrunk.Sim
short: Virtual Execution Environment for simulation and impact forecasting.
description: HoneyDrunk.Sim is the Grid's simulation layer: a sandboxed Virtual Execution Environment that models the impact of proposed decisions before they touch reality. Sim runs deterministic what-if evaluations across nodes, dependencies, policies, and environments, producing structured evidence like blast radius, risk posture, cost projections, and failure-mode forecasts. It does not govern, advise, or execute. It generates evidence for Governor to weigh and for Clarity to explain.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Sim is the Grid's simulation layer: a sandboxed Virtual Execution Environment that models the impact of proposed decisions before they touch reality. Sim runs deterministic what-if evaluations across nodes, dependencies, policies, and environments, producing structured evidence like blast radius, risk posture, cost projections, and failure-mode forecasts.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provide a safe, sandboxed environment to evaluate proposed changes without impacting production. Generate structured evidence that supports governance decisions and operational approvals. Standardize how the Hive models risk, blast radius, and cost before execution. Enable repeatable simulations that can be compared over time and across environments. Reduce incident risk by catching unsafe or high-impact changes before they are applied.
long_description.primary_audience: Governor (for deliberation evidence), Operator (for operational confidence), Clarity (for human explanation), and internal systems requiring impact validation before execution.
value_props: Accept simulation requests describing a proposed change, policy, rollout, or configuration shift.
Resolve relevant dependency graphs and environmental constraints from Grid manifests and registry state.
Run deterministic evaluation passes to estimate impact vectors (latency, error rate risk, cost, capacity, dependency health).
Produce a versioned Simulation Report with explicit assumptions, confidence, and identified failure modes.
Emit telemetry to Pulse for simulation runs, durations, and outcomes without leaking customer content.
Support replay and comparison of prior simulations for trend-aware forecasting.
does_not_do: Does not govern or make decisions.
Does not advise or explain outcomes to humans directly.
Does not execute, deploy, or apply changes.
Does not flip flags or modify production state.
Sim produces evidence. Governor decides. Clarity explains. Operator acts.
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal infrastructure. Value measured in reduced incident risk, faster governance cycles, and confident operational decisions-not direct revenue.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Phase 1: Core simulation engine with dependency graph resolution and deterministic impact scoring. Phase 2: Scenario libraries, environment drift modeling, and historical Pulse integration. Phase 3: Resilience forecasting, multi-branch comparison, and Governor-integrated deliberation workflows.
long_description.grid_relationship: Governor: Sim provides evidence for Governor deliberation-blast radius, risk scores, and failure modes. Governor weighs this evidence to make decisions. Clarity: Sim produces raw simulation data; Clarity narrates it for humans. Operator: Sim validates proposed operational changes before Operator applies them. Pulse: Sim emits telemetry and consumes historical signals for trend-aware modeling.
long_description.demo_path: RFC submitted -> Governor requests Sim simulation -> Sim resolves dependency graph -> Runs deterministic impact evaluation -> Produces Simulation Report with blast radius, risk posture, cost projection -> Governor weighs evidence -> Clarity explains results to human -> Operator applies approved change with confidence.
long_description.signal_quote: Model reality before you touch it.
long_description.vision: A modeling lab for the Hive: every meaningful change can be simulated, scored, and compared before execution. Sim becomes the standard source of evidence for blast radius and risk, enabling Governor to deliberate with receipts and Operator to act with confidence.
